rationale_overall: All five current holdings remain firmly thesis-aligned at year-end 2023 and none has hit a peak-enthusiasm or thesis-breach signal that would justify burning the cap-5 swap. NVDA is mid-surge on the generative-AI data-center buildout (Q3 FY24 revenue ~$18B, data-center segment up ~3x YoY), still well inside the 'ride AI' window before any trim. URA rides a strong nuclear buildup: uranium spot broke above $90/lb in late 2023 (a 15-year high) and the COP28 declaration in Dec 2023 saw 22 countries pledge to triple nuclear capacity by 2050 — squarely the SMR/near-term nuclear thesis. LNG's Cheniere keeps printing cash on sustained European demand for US gas through the second winter of the Russia-Ukraine war. RKLB and IONQ are early-stage rockets and quantum bets matching the profile's explicit 'next waves' list; both are pre-inflection but the thesis is exposure, not timing. The one named wave not represented is robotics, but no single robotics ticker had a discrete commercial milestone in Q4 2023 strong enough to justify swapping out a thesis-aligned holding at cap=5. Patience this quarter.
